definition: An invisible force that pulls objects toward Earth and other large objects in space.
simpleVersion: The force that makes things fall down.
example: Gravity is why the apple fell from the tree.

definition: A push or pull that makes something move or change direction.
example: Gravity is a force. A kick is also a force.

definition: To move around something else in a circular path.
simpleVersion: To go around something in a circle.
example: The Moon orbits Earth. Planets orbit the Sun.

definition: How heavy something is. Weight depends on gravity.
example: You weigh more on Earth than you would on the Moon.

definition: When something speeds up, slows down, or changes direction.
example: When gravity pulls a falling apple downward faster and faster, that's acceleration.

definition: A famous scientist who discovered that gravity is a force.
example: Isaac Newton figured out why the apple fell.

definition: A large object that orbits a star.
simpleVersion: A big round object that goes around the Sun.
example: Earth is a planet. Mars is another planet.

definition: A smaller object that orbits a planet.
simpleVersion: Something that goes around a planet.
example: Earth has one Moon. It orbits Earth and pulls on our oceans.
